notes: All ingredient amounts are for base_yield. To bake a different batch size, multiply every ingredient amount by (target / base_yield.amount) when using the same unit (e.g. 48 cookies → multiply by 48/24 = 2).
Rest dough at least 30 minutes for better flavor; overnight is best.

ingredients.name: light brown sugar
notes: Pack firmly into the measuring cup.

substitutions.name: dark chocolate chunks
notes: Roughly chop a bar for irregular pockets of chocolate.

steps.step: In a large bowl, beat butter with granulated and brown sugars until light and fluffy, 2–3 minutes.
notes: Scrape the bowl once halfway through.

steps.step: Beat in the egg and vanilla until fully combined.

haccp.control_point: Use pasteurized eggs if serving to vulnerable populations; otherwise minimize time raw egg sits at room temperature.

steps.step: In a separate bowl, whisk flour, baking soda, and salt.
notes: Whisk thoroughly so baking soda is evenly distributed.

steps.step: Stir dry ingredients into the butter mixture until just combined; do not overmix.
notes: A few streaks of flour are fine before adding chips.

steps.step: Fold in chocolate chips (or substitution) until evenly distributed.
Cover and chill dough at least 30 minutes, or up to 48 hours.
Preheat the oven and line baking sheets with parchment. Portion dough into mounds; leave space for spread.
notes: A #40 scoop (~1.5 tbsp) works well for uniform cookies.

steps.step: Bake until edges are set and centers look slightly underdone; cool on the sheet 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack.

haccp.critical_control_point: Cookies are safe when baked to a visibly set edge; for food-service settings, verify baked goods cooling and storage within 2 hours of leaving the oven.

source_book.title: Home Baking: A Field Guide
notes: First edition; technique chapter referenced for creaming method.
